items.title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
items.summary: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.

items.title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025

items.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
items.summary: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.

items.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
items.summary: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.

items.title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
items.summary: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.

items.title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
items.summary: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.

items.title: Trump asked Musk for SpaceX stock to seed US kids’ savings accounts, report says
items.summary: Sources suggest Musk may be mulling big donation to Trump Accounts.

items.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
items.summary: South Korea targets physical AI lead and commercial humanoid robots by 2028.

items.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
items.summary: Half-million-strong military will train on drones as “universal combat tool.”

items.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
items.summary: NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.

items.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
items.summary: Notion is "going all in on using agents to run your inbox."

items.title: Google finally releases a Finance Android app, promises iOS version later in 2026

items.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
items.summary: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.
